name: Service account has user-managed key older than 90 days
description: A service account has a user-managed key that was created more than 90 days ago. Long-lived credentials increase the window of opportunity for compromise. User-managed keys are high-risk because they can be downloaded and do not rotate automatically.
remediation: Rotate service account keys every 90 days or less. Better yet, eliminate user-managed keys entirely: use Workload Identity for GKE, Attached Service Accounts for VMs, and impersonation for local development. Delete keys that are no longer in use.
